Motion To Release Charging Lien For Intentional Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, David J. Stock of the law offices of Rankin, Stock appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Diana Lee (Email: dianamay567@outlook.com; Tel: 702.505.3402) appears as "power of attorney" for defendant/moving party, Steve Lee

Attorneys, Todd D. Reeves and Sargis Atanous (Email: injurylaw@dunnion.com) of the law offices of The Dunnion Law Firm (Tel: 831.373.8035) appears as counsel for co-defendant/opposing party, The Dunnion Law Firm

Court and counsel discuss Diana Lee's appearances and legality of her representing defendant Steve Lee)

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT STEVE LEE's Motion To Release Charging Lien For Intentional Breach Of Fiduciary Duty is DENIED without prejudice. Diana Lee is attempting to unlawfully practice law under the guise of power of attorney. "A power of attorney does not permit an agent to act as an attorney at law. If the rule were otherwise, the State Bar Act could be relegated to contempt by any layman who secured from his principal an ordinary power of attorney, for the purpose of representing him in pending litigation." (People By and Through Dept. of Public Works v. Malone (1965) 232 Cal.App.2d 531, 536-537.) "By definition, one cannot appear in 'propria' persona for another person." (Drake v. Superior Court (1994) 21 Cal.App.4th 1826, 1830 [holding that trial court properly refused to allow attorney in fact holding power of attorney for pro per litigant to appear at hearing and ordered brief stricken].) In any event, defendant Lee fails to present sufficient competent evidence to support release of the lien. The parties may renew these issues at the trial or in a separate arbitration, if one is ordered on appropriate motion. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 386(e) ["Except in cases where by the law a right to a jury trial is now given, conflicting claims to funds or property or the value thereof so deposited or delivered shall be deemed issues triable by the court, and such issues may be first tried."].)

Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
